evidence.scientific_clarity: Dual-axis time-series clearly shows log-scale loss declining from ~2.19 to ~0.21 and cosine LR on right axis; brief's claim fully carried.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 set; pdffonts confirms Type 3 bitmap 'DejaVuSans' — default Matplotlib font, not editable TrueType/Type42.
evidence.palette: Blue (#1f77b4) for loss, red (#d62728) dashed for LR — colorblind-safe pair; axis tick labels color-coupled to series per brief hint.
evidence.layout_density: 7×4 inch figure with tight_layout; good panel use, no whitespace gaps, no label overlaps, y-ranges sensible (log loss, LR≥0).
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure accurately; legend upper-right with framealpha; axis colors coupled — minor gap: no gridlines on log axis for precise reading.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is vector (Matplotlib pdf backend) but uses Type 3 bitmap fonts, not Type 42; text is not selectable/editable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf, figure.png, caption.tex, gen_figure.py; bonus figure.svg also generated.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names both series, cites specific numerical range (2.19→0.21), explains log scale, ×10⁻³ scaling, cosine annealing, and color-coding of axes.

overall_strengths: Clean dual-axis design with color-coupled tick labels exactly as the brief requested, making the figure immediately readable.
Caption is publication-quality: specific numerical finding, explains visual encoding, and matches figure content precisely.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype='none' for SVG): PDF embeds Type 3 bitmap fonts instead of editable TrueType, breaking vector text editability.
Default DejaVu Sans font family used — no switch to a publication-grade sans-serif (e.g. Helvetica, Arial, or a math-ready font like STIXTwo).
